## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about when you should use Advantage Plus placements. Let's go! Should you use Advantage Plus placements? When you create an ad set, Advantage Plus placements is on by default. This makes all placements available for your ads. I know that many advertisers will remove certain placements to focus on what works best. It makes sense in theory. Here's what I recommend. If you're optimizing for any type of conversion, leave Advantage Plus placements on. Matt's only goal in that case is getting you the most conversions as efficiently as possible. Turning off placements could make that more difficult. The primary reason to turn off placements is due to low quality actions. The algorithm doesn't know what a low quality click is, for example. It doesn't matter if they're accidental clicks, click fraud, or bots. Lots of that can be found within audience network. So why not turn off audience network when optimizing for conversions? Because if conversions won't come from that placement, the algorithm will adjust. But if you optimize for link clicks or landing page views, expect most of your budget to be spent there. Overall, don't overthink it, especially when optimizing for a conversion. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate, and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
